prompt: A strict, authentic Bauhaus graphic design composition from the 1920s. The image features a stark, thick, perfectly straight diagonal black line cutting through the center of the framed space. On the left side of the black line is a scattered group of solid, flat primary-red circles. On the right side of the line is a scattered group of flat primary-blue squares. The thick black line perfectly separates the circles from the squares. Completely flat, unmodulated colors, no 3D shading, strict geometric minimalism, printed on a slightly aged, textured cream-colored paper. Absolutely no text, no numbers, no labels.
tradition_approach: I mapped the logic of an SVM to Bauhaus grammar by using strictly flat primary shapes (red circles/blue squares) for the data classes, separated by the movement's signature thick, stark black diagonal line as the hyperplane.
